title: Caroxylon dolichostigmum
taxonomy: Caroxylon dolichostigmum is classified within the family Amaranthaceae. This classification places it within the order Caryophyllales. It further belongs to the class Magnoliopsida and the division Magnoliophyta. The kingdom it belongs to is Plantae, indicating its status as a flowering plant. It is also classified as a monocot.

intro: Caroxylon dolichostigmum, also known as the "saltwort" or "sea purslane," is a succulent plant species that belongs to the Amaranthaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Caroxylon dolichostigmum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditional herbal preparations made from Caroxylon dolichostigmum include infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and wounds.
